frontierComparison.interpretation: The 20-bucket post-next q-avoiding batch cover consumed its finite source token with no survivors, but emitted a larger 22 successor bucket surface and a much larger successor q-avoiding boundary. This assembly does not claim a global decrease; it consumes the immediate post-cover decision obligation by selecting a whole 22-bucket compression/ranked-boundary action before singleton q-child descent.

assembledPieces.claim: All 718 source rows and all 2,946,810,455,708,641,575,397,311 post-next q-avoiding source classes from the 20-bucket post-next token have a later square-obstruction child by prime 263.
assembledPieces.boundary: This closes the source token as a no-survivor batch classification, but it emits successor root-child and successor q-avoiding rank obligations.

assembledPieces.claim: The successor square-obstruction children are partitioned into 22 buckets q in {139,149,151,157,163,167,173,179,181,191,193,197,199,211,223,227,229,233,239,241,251,263}, each with exactly two roots per source class.
assembledPieces.boundary: The 22 buckets are exact successor boundaries, not terminal closures.

assembledPieces.claim: The batch cover emits 5,893,620,911,417,283,150,794,622 successor root children.
assembledPieces.boundary: Those root children remain outside the assembled proof until a later packet compresses, closes, blocks, or ranks them.

assembledPieces.claim: The same 22 successor buckets emit 111,172,518,226,866,898,571,161,320,153 successor q-avoiding classes.
assembledPieces.boundary: Those classes require a compression theorem, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or deterministic ranked boundary before any singleton q-child descent.

supportedClaim: For the selected p443/q97/p479-available branch, the 20-bucket post-next q-avoiding batch cover is consumed with no source survivors. The live successor surface is now the whole 22-bucket successor rank boundary, so the next theorem step must compress or deterministically boundary that whole surface before q139, q149, or any other singleton child is opened.

compressionCandidate.name: p443_q97_p479_later_prime_15_bucket_next_prime_17_bucket_post_next_20_bucket_successor_22_bucket_rank_compression
compressionCandidate.coveredFamily: All 22 successor obstruction-prime buckets emitted by the 20-bucket post-next q-avoiding batch cover: 718 source rows, 2,946,810,455,708,641,575,397,311 source post-next q-avoiding classes, 5,893,620,911,417,283,150,794,622 successor root children, and 111,172,518,226,866,898,571,161,320,153 successor q-avoiding classes.
compressionCandidate.whyThisCompresses: The q-avoiding cover proves row-uniform first successor obstruction and a shared two-root law for every stored source row. The remaining theorem-facing object is therefore the 22-prime successor rank surface, not any one q-child.
compressionCandidate.whyNotSingletonDescent: A q139, q149, or later singleton descent would consume only one emitted bucket while the frontier has expanded to 58 obligations. The finite token now available is the whole 22-bucket successor rank boundary.
compressionCandidate.failureBoundary: If no common compression theorem or impossibility theorem is visible, emit an exact deterministic 22-bucket ranked boundary packet with all buckets accounted, explicit survivors if any, and the next finite token.

remainingPuzzleBoundary.exactBoundary: The next theorem surface is the 22 successor bucket family: 5,893,620,911,417,283,150,794,622 successor root children and 111,172,518,226,866,898,571,161,320,153 successor q-avoiding classes.

oneNextAction.action: Compress the 22 successor obstruction-prime buckets into theorem-facing families, prove a structural rank decrease or impossibility theorem, or emit an exact deterministic 22-bucket ranked boundary before singleton q-child descent.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: A deterministic ranked boundary packet with all 22 buckets accounted, explicit survivors if any, and the next finite token; no singleton q-child descent is opened by this assembly.
oneNextAction.whyCheaperThanSingleSelector: The 22 buckets share the same row-uniform two-root mechanism and are exact at the rank-token level, so a whole-boundary compression/rank packet is more informative than starting q139/q149/etc. one by one.
oneNextAction.completionRule: The next packet accounts for every one of the 22 successor buckets by compression theorem, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or exact deterministic ranked boundary.

proofBoundary: This packet is convergence assembly after the 20-bucket post-next q-avoiding batch cover. It chooses a whole 22-bucket successor compression/ranked-boundary action and blocks singleton q-child descent. It does not close any emitted successor bucket, root child, q-avoiding class, unavailable complement, p4217 complement, collision-free matching obligation, or Problem 848.
nextTheoremMove: Compress the 22 successor rank buckets, prove a structural/impossibility theorem over the whole successor surface, or emit an exact deterministic 22-bucket ranked boundary; do not open q139/q149/singleton descent first.
